findings: Average Packet Size exhibits low cardinality with class-conditioned structural variation.
Backward packet length features including Avg Bwd Segment Size and Bwd Packet Length Max show low cardinality and near-constant behavior in some features, alongside class-conditioned variation.
The tension between near-constant low cardinality features and features with strong class-conditioned distributional shifts is locally preserved.
contradictions: No local contradictions detected; tension between low cardinality and class variation remains unresolved but preserved.
limitations: Distribution verification was not performed due to budget constraints, limiting full quantification of distribution shifts.

findings: Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) exhibit strong class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and high class separation signals.

Average Packet Size shows class-conditioned structural variation with moderate variance ratio and differing unique value counts between classes as per local context, though direct distribution verification was not performed.
A local unresolved tension exists between features with near-constant values and those with strong class-conditioned variation.
limitations: No direct distribution verification was performed on Average Packet Size within this task scope.

findings: Backward bulk-related features with near-constant values exhibit high local label conflicts, indicating saturation or measurement limits rather than meaningful class variation.
Local evidence preserves the tension between low cardinality features and other features with strong class-conditioned variation.
No local consistency patterns verified for these low cardinality features, supporting the saturation/measurement limit interpretation.
limitations: Analysis limited to backward bulk features; forward bulk and other features not locally verified in this task.

findings: Features e8 to e12 and e33 show strong class-conditioned structural variation and distributional shifts, indicating heterogeneous behavior sensitive to class labels.
Features e23 to e32 form clusters of near-constant, low-cardinality features with minimal variability and no finite pairwise dependencies, representing stable but weak signals.
Dependency contextualization on 'Bwd Packet Length Max' confirmed concentrated dependency patterns consistent with class-conditioned variation.
Attempts to contextualize dependencies for low-cardinality features failed due to lack of finite pairwise dependencies, confirming their near-constant nature.
The tension between low-cardinality stable features and high-variation features is preserved and characterized locally without cross-cluster relation verification due to bounded context constraints.
